title: artemisia campestris var. pacifica
intro: Artemisia campestris var. pacifica, commonly known as Western Mugwort, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family that is native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Northwest. Western Mugwort has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbalism, where it has been valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits associated with Western Mugwort include relief from digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to act as a natural insect repellent. In herbal preparations, Western Mugwort is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it can also be smoked or added to bath waters for therapeutic purposes.
